item.title: Academic

item.description:
About me

I am an Associate Professor at Saint Louis University in the Department of Pharmacology and Physiology. My research interests span different areas including molecular biology, structural biology, and bioinformatics. To learn more about my “peer-reviewed” research projects, please visit the “Publication” page. To learn more about my unpublished research interests, please visit the “Recent Posts”” area.

item.title: Recent Posts
Selected Publications
Projects
Recent & Upcoming Talks
item.description:
• Graphs Galore! Representing Knowledge in the Sciences and Humanities
    Carleton College. October 22, 2019. (Slides)
    Macalester College. November 1, 2019. (Slides)

item.title: Recent Publications
Teaching
item.description:
I teach the following courses at Macalester. Some of my courses have course websites which you can navigate to below. For many of my classes I make videos, which can be found on my YouTube channel.

  • STAT 125: Epidemiology
  • STAT 155: Introduction to Statistical Modeling
    • Class notes in collaboration with Brianna Heggeseth
    • [S20 Site]
  • STAT 253: Statistical Machine Learning
    • [S19 Site]
  • STAT 451: Causal Inference (formerly STAT 394)
    • [S20 Site]

item.title: CV
item.description: My CV is available in HTML or PDF form. (Last updated: June 6, 2020)

item.title: Fun
item.description:
I am an avid Dungeons and Dragons player and game enthusiast, and I love finding ways to bring coding and analysis into these beloved hobbies of mine. As a result, I have a few side projects devoted entirely to fun!

  • Map Annotator: A Shiny app that I developed to create clickable annotated maps for helping game masters keep track of details in information-dense areas.
  • R + Magic the Gathering: (Slides) A foray into the rvest package in R with an application for organizing information about Magic the Gathering decklists.

item.title: Contact
Tags
COVID-19 situation in the US (3/1/2020-6/12/2020)
item.description:
import os
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

We download the most updated Cases in the US from the “COVID-19 Data Repository by the Center for Systems Science and Engineering (CSSE)” at Johns Hopkins University. These include (i) confirmed cases and (ii) deaths, since 1/23/20 till the preceding day of the current date.

os.getcwd()
%cd ../COVID-19/
! git pull origin master
! git remote -v

/media/data_analysis5/apps/git_repository/jupyter_notebook/kaggle/covid_19/COVID-19
From https://github.com/CSSEGISandData/COVID-19
 * branch              master     -> FETCH_HEAD
Already up to date.
origin  https://github.com/CSSEGISandData/COVID-19.git (fetch)
origin  https://github.com/CSSEGISandData/COVID-19.git (push)

df_death=pd.read_csv("/media/data_analysis5/apps/git_repository/jupyter_notebook/kaggle/covid_19/COVID-19/csse_covid_19_data\
/csse_covid_19_time_series/time_series_covid19_deaths_US.csv")


df_confirmed=pd.read_csv("/media/data_analysis5/apps/git_repository/jupyter_notebook/kaggle/covid_19/COVID-19/csse_covid_19_data\
/csse_covid_19_time_series/time_series_covid19_confirmed_US.csv")

state_name=df_death["Province_State"].unique()
state_name=state_name.tolist()
state_name.append("all")

We define a class “covid19” to store the case data and the derived statistics. It includes accumulated and daily confirmed cases and deaths, methods to calculate these numbers and to plot the statistics.

class covid19:
    country="US"
    def __init__(self,name):
        self.state_name=name
        self.df_death=None
        self.df_confirm=None
        self.df_death_accu=None
        self.df_confirm_dccu=None
        self.df_death_daily=None
        self.df_confirm_daily=None
        
    def update_df(self):
        if self.state_name=="all":
            self.df_confirm=df_confirmed
            self.df_death=df_death
        else:
            self.df_death=df_death[df_death["Province_State"]==self.state_name]
            self.df_confirm=df_confirmed[df_confirmed["Province_State"]==self.state_name]  
        
    def get_accu(self):
        self.df_confirm_accu=self.df_confirm.iloc[:,12:]
        self.df_confirm_accu=self.df_confirm_accu.sum(axis=0)
        self.df_confirm_accu.index=pd.to_datetime(self.df_confirm_accu.index)
        self.df_confirm_daily=self.df_confirm_accu.diff().fillna(self.df_confirm_accu[0]).astype(np.int64)
        self.df_confirm_daily.index=pd.to_datetime(self.df_confirm_daily.index)
    def get_death(self):
        self.df_death_accu=self.df_death.iloc[:,12:]
        self.df_death_accu=self.df_death_accu.sum(axis=0)
        self.df_death_accu.index=pd.to_datetime(self.df_death_accu.index)
        self.df_death_daily=self.df_death_accu.diff().fillna(self.df_death_accu[0]).astype(np.int64)
        self.df_death_daily.index=pd.to_datetime(self.df_death_daily.index)
    
    def update_all_plots(self):
        self.update_df()
        self.get_accu()
        self.get_death()
        self.get_plots()
    def get_plots(self):
        figure, axes = plt.subplots(ncols=4,nrows=1,figsize=(20, 5))
        plt.setp(axes, xlim=  pd.to_datetime(["2020-03-01","2020-06-15"]).to_numpy())
#         fig, axs = plt.subplots(ncols=4, figsize=(18, 10))
        axes[0].plot(self.df_confirm_accu)
        axes[0].title.set_text(self.state_name)
        axes[0].set_ylabel("accumulated confirmed cases")
        axes[1].plot(self.df_death_accu)
        axes[1].set_ylabel("daily confirmed cases")
        axes[2].plot(self.df_confirm_daily)
        axes[2].set_ylabel("accumulated deaths")
        axes[3].plot(self.df_death_daily)
        axes[3].set_ylabel("daily deaths")
        figure.autofmt_xdate(rotation=45)
        figure.tight_layout()

plt.rcParams.update({'figure.max_open_warning': 0}) # suppress warnings.
for x in state_name:
    temp=covid19(x)
    temp.update_all_plots()
plt.show()

item.title: GPR160 de-orphanization reveals critical roles in neuropathic pain in rodents
Histone deacetylase 3 preferentially binds and collaborates with the transcription factor RUNX1 to repress AML1-ETO- dependent transcription in t(8;21) AML
The transcriptional corepressor CBFA2T3 inhibits all-trans retinoic acid-induced myeloid gene expression and differentiation in acute myeloid leukemia
Circadian clock protein Rev-erbα regulates neuroinflammation
LXR-inverse agonism stimulates immune-mediated tumor destruction by enhancing CD8 T-cell activity in triple negative breast cancer
Low plasma adropin concentrations increase risks of weight gain and metabolic dysregulation in response to a high-sugar diet in male nonhuman primates
Myeloid translocation gene CBFA2T3 directs a relapse gene program and determines patient-specific outcomes in AML
Pharmacological activation of the nuclear receptor REV-ERB reverses cognitive deficits and reduces amyloid-β burden in a mouse model of Alzheimer's disease
Adropin: An endocrine link between the biological clock and cholesterol homeostasis

Epigenetic regulator ARID1A and stem cell transcription factor SOX9 in the maintenance of pancreatic ductal cell differentiation state and development of intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasia (IPMN) and pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC)
A FOXO1-induced oncogenic network defines the AML1-ETO preleukemic program
EZH2-, CHD4-, and IDH-linked epigenetic perturbation and its association with survival in glioma patients
Emerging Roles of MTG16 in Cell-Fate Control of Hematopoietic Stem Cells and Cancer
Rev-Erb co-regulates muscle regeneration via tethered interaction with the NF-Y cistrome
Rev-erb regulation of cholesterologenesis
Transcriptional and Genomic Control of Stem Cells in Development and Cancer
New insights into transcriptional and leukemogenic mechanisms of AML1-ETO and E2A fusion proteins
The optimal corepressor function of nuclear receptor corepressor (NCoR) for peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor γ requires g protein pathway suppressor 2
Differential involvement of E2A-corepressor interactions in distinct leukemogenic pathways
A TAF4 coactivator function for E proteins that involves enhanced TFIID binding
DNA Homologous Recombination Factor SFR1 Physically and Functionally Interacts with Estrogen Receptor Alpha
The novel interaction between microspherule protein Msp58 and ubiquitin E3 ligase EDD regulates cell cycle progression
Regulated clearance of histone deacetylase 3 protects independent formation of nuclear receptor corepressor complexes
LMO7 mediates cell-specific activation of the rho-myocardin-related transcription factor-serum response factor pathway and plays an important role in breast cancer cell migration
The leukemogenicity of AML1-ETO is dependent on site-specific lysine acetylation
Identification of ASF/SF2 as a critical, allele-specific effector of the cyclin D1b oncogene
Multivalent binding of the ETO corepressor to E proteins facilitates dual repression controls targeting chromatin and the basal transcription machinery
Vav3 oncogene activates estrogen receptor and its overexpression may be involved in human breast cancer
The acute myeloid leukemia fusion protein AML1-ETO targets E proteins via a paired amphipathic helix-like TBP-associated factor homology domain
E protein silencing by the leukemogenic AML1-ETO fusion protein
Cooperative function of Aml1-ETO corepressor recruitment domains in the expansion of primary bone marrow cells
The N-CoR-HDAC3 nuclear receptor corepressor complex inhibits the JNK pathway through the integral subunit GPS2
Oligomerization of ETO is obligatory for corepressor interaction
Nuclear receptor corepressors partner with class II histone deacetylases in a Sin3-independent repression pathway
The mechanism of action of thyroid hormones
A novel role for helix 12 of retinoid X receptor in regulating repression
Aberrant recruitment of the nuclear receptor corepressor-histone deacetylase complex by the acute myeloid leukemia fusion partner ETO
Proteasomal regulation of nuclear receptor corepressor-mediated repression
Differential recognition of liganded and unliganded thyroid hormone receptor by retinoid X receptor regulates transcriptional repression
Stoichiometric and steric principles governing repression by nuclear hormone receptors
DNA-independent and DNA-dependent mechanisms regulate the differential heterodimerization of the isoforms of the thyroid hormone receptor with retinoid X receptor
The mechanism of action of trichosanthin on eukaryotic ribosomes - RNA N-glycosidase activity of the cytotoxin

item.description:
Leslie Myint

  • e: lmyint@macalester.edu
  • w: lmyint.github.io

Education

PhD in Biostatistics May 2018

Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health

Dissertation: Evidence-Based Methods in Studies of Biology and Data Analysis

Advisor: Kasper Daniel Hansen


BS in Biomedical Engineering May 2013

Johns Hopkins University

Secondary major: Applied Mathematics and Statistics
Minor: Computer Science


Work Experience

Assistant Professor August 2018 - present

Department of Mathematics, Statistics, and Computer Science

Macalester College, Saint Paul, MN


Johns Hopkins Biostatistics Center July 2016 - August 2017

Student statistical consultant

Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health, Baltimore, MD

Advisor: Carol Thompson, MS


Siemens Competition 2016 - 2017

Stage I, II, and finalist judge

Categories: Computer Science, Mathematics, Bioinformatics, Cell/Cancer Biology, and Genetics

Publications

Published

  1. Myint L, Hadavand A, Jager L, Leek J. 2019. Comparison of Beginning R Students’ Perceptions of Peer-Made Plots Created in Two Plotting Systems: A Randomized Experiment. Journal of Statistics Education 28:98–108. DOI: 10.1080/10691898.2019.1695554.
  2. Myint L, Wang R, Boukas L, Hansen KD, Goff LA, Avramopoulos D. 2020b. A screen of 1,049 schizophrenia and 30 Alzheimer’s‐associated variants for regulatory potential. American Journal of Medical Genetics Part B: Neuropsychiatric Genetics 183:61–73. DOI: 10.1002/ajmg.b.32761.
  3. Myint L, Avramopoulos DG, Goff LA, Hansen KD. 2019. Linear models enable powerful differential activity analysis in massively parallel reporter assays. BMC Genomics 20:209. DOI: 10.1186/s12864-019-5556-x.
  4. Myint L, Leek JT, Jager LR. 2018. Explanation of observational data engenders a causal belief about smoking and cancer. PeerJ 6:e5597. DOI: 10.7717/peerj.5597.
    • Press:
      Preprint was featured in the February 2018 issue of Significance Magazine.
  5. Monroe AK, Myint L, M Rutstein R, Aberg J, L Boswell S, L Agwu A, A Gebo K, D Moore R, HIV Research Network. 2018. Factors Associated With Gaps in Medicaid Enrollment Among People With HIV and the Effect of Gaps on Viral Suppression. Journal of Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndromes 78:413–420. DOI: 10.1097/QAI.0000000000001702.
  6. Kang JY, Rabiei AH, Myint L, Nei M. 2017. Equivocal significance of post-ictal generalized EEG suppression as a marker of SUDEP risk. Seizure: The Journal of the British Epilepsy Association 48:28–32. DOI: 10.1016/j.seizure.2017.03.017.
  7. Myint L, Kleensang A, Zhao L, Hartung T, Hansen KD. 2017. Joint Bounding of Peaks Across Samples Improves Differential Analysis in Mass Spectrometry-Based Metabolomics. Analytical Chemistry 89:3517–3523. DOI: 10.1021/acs.analchem.6b04719.

Software

yamss: Tools for the analysis of high-throughput metabolomics data. An R package released through the Bioconductor project.
https://www.bioconductor.org/packages/yamss

mpra: Tools for the analysis of data from massively parallel reporter assays. An R package released through the Bioconductor project.
https://www.bioconductor.org/packages/mpra

Grant Participation

Metabolomics of Uremic Symptoms in Dialysis Patients
NIH R01 subaward contract
Dates: 6/1/2019 - 5/31/2021
Role: statistical analyst

Presentations

Graphs Galore! Representing Knowledge in the Sciences and Humanities
Invited Talk: Carleton College (slides)
October 2019
Talk: Macalester College (slides)
November 2019

Statistical methods for querying the regulatory role of DNA
Invited Talk: Creighton University Math Colloquium (slides)
March 2019

Magical Web Scraping with rvest
Invited Talk: Baltimore R Ladies Group (slides)
May 2018

Joint Preprocessing of Samples Improves Power in Differential Analysis for Mass Spectrometry-Based Metabolomics
Invited Talk: JHU Biophysics
December 2017

Shiny Applications for Teaching and Dungeons and Dragons
Invited Talk: Baltimore UseR Group (slides)
September 2017

A Method for Joint Processing of Mass Spectrometry-Based Metabolomics Data for Improved Differential Analysis
Poster: ENAR, Washington D.C.
March 2017

Teaching

Macalester College

Instructor
  • STAT 125: Epidemiology (F19)
  • STAT 155: Introduction to Statistical Modeling (F18, S20)
  • STAT 253: Statistical Machine Learning (S19)
  • STAT 394: Causal Inference (S20)

Cloud-Based Data Science Specialization

Content developer

A massive open online course on the Leanpub platform for providing a highly accessible data science education. Content developer for the following courses:

  • Organizing Data Science Projects
  • Version Control
  • Introduction to R
  • Data Tidying

Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health

Instructor
  • Statistical Thinking for Informed Decision Making (2 semesters)
    I developed this course as part of the Gordis Teaching Fellowship, a school-wide award that provides funds to design and teach an undergraduate class. A news article-motivated introduction to major biostatistical areas, including causal inference, survey sampling, and survival analysis.

Teaching Assistant
  • Public Health Biostatistics (3 semesters)
  • Introduction to R for Public Health Researchers (1 course)
  • Statistical Methods in Public Health (3 quarters)
  • Data Analysis Workshop (2 courses)
  • Statistics for Genomics (1 quarter)
  • Statistics for Laboratory Scientists (2 quarters)
  • Summer Institute: Statistical Reasoning in Public Health (2 courses)

Tutor
  • Statistical Methods in Public Health (2 quarters)
  • Mentor for Center for Talented Youth Cogito Research Award Recipient (3 months)

Johns Hopkins University

Teaching Assistant
  • Introduction to Java (1 semester)

Awards

Helen Abbey Award May 2017

Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health

Excellence in teaching (website)


Professional Service

Memberships

  • American Statistical Association
  • Eastern North American Region of the International Biometric Society

Conferences

  • 2020: Organizer and Chair for an invited session for the Education Track at the Symposium on Data Science and Statistics

Reviewer

  • 2020: Open Case Studies
  • 2020: Journal of Statistics Education
  • 2019: Nature Human Behaviour
  • 2019: Technology Innovations in Statistics Education
  • 2019: Journal of Statistics Education
  • 2018: BiOverlay
  • 2018: American Journal of Epidemiology
  • 2017: Observational Studies
